rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are visible in the current scope or properly qualified.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the correct scalar types (int, real, bool).
Remove invalid guarantee clauses when root identifiers are unresolved or expressions have <error> type.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are visible in the current AADL scope.
Use only declared constants or visible features in AGREE expressions to avoid type resolution errors.
Validate that all AGREE type declarations match supported scalar types (int, real, bool).
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation scopes; use component type scopes for contract assumptions and guarantees.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Guarantee expressions must evaluate to boolean types.
Property references in AGREE must resolve to valid definitions in the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new property definitions or constants that are not visible in the current AADL scope.
Do not place invalid or unresolved identifiers in AGREE guarantee or equation declarations.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about unreachable or non-existent property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about missing annex placement or assume/guarantee syntax
Component type and implementation structure extraction
